name: Fish Fillets with Tomatoes Capers & Olives
recipeIngredient: Fish broth, bottled clam juice or water salt (opt)
recipeInstructions:
like boneless and skinless red snapper or halibut,
in 6 1 1/2-inch thick piece.  plus a few sprigs for
garnish
1. Rinse fillets, place them in a non aluminum dish and
sprinkle them with lime juice and salt. Cover and
refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. In a large nonstick skillet, heat oils over medium heat,
add onions and cook, stirring frequently, until golden,
about 5 to 7 minutes.
3. Cut tomatoes into 1 inch pieces and place them in a bowl,
collecting the juices as you go.
4. Add garlic to onion and cook for a minute or so, then
add tomatoes. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes, or until most of
the liquid has evaporated.
5. Divide olives and capers between two small bowls and set one
aside for garnishes. To the other bowl, add jalapenos,
pickling juice, marjoram, thyme and chopped parsley.
6. Add olive-herb mixture to tomatoes, along with fish stock,
clam juice or water. Simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, then
taste, adding salt if necessary.
7. Remove cheesecloth bag, if using, and bay leaves.
8. Remove fillets from marinade and rinse.
9. To cook on the stovetop, place fillets in the skillet,
covering them well with sauce. Cover and cook over medium heat
for 4 minutes. Turn fillets over, cover and cook for 2 to 3
minutes longer, until they flake when pressed firmly with a
fork at the thickest part.
10. Alternatively, preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Place fillets
in a single layer in a lightly oiled baking dish. Spoon sauce
over them, cover with aluminum foil and bake for 10 to 15 minutes,
or just until fish flakes when pressed firmly with a fork at the
thickest part.
185 CALORIES PER SERVING: 25 G PROTEIN, 5 G FAT, 10 G CARBO- HYDRATE;
249 MG SODIUM; 41 MG CHOLESTEROL.
